mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts or clear easing coincide with stronger forward returns than unchanged-policy periods.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising policy rates, or if the sign of the relation is negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent trend identifies labor-market deterioration that reduces expected consumer income and corporate sales, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should appear only after a clear unemployment break, not during stable low-unemployment periods.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns dominate after a 0.3pp/3m unemployment rise for any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in RSAFS and HOUST signals resilient final demand and housing-led spending, which supports earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism requires both consumer spending and housing activity to improve together; either series alone is insufficient.
failure_condition: Reject if joint-positive RSAFS and HOUST months do not outperform other months on 60-day forward SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the signal better than their interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO with no offsetting labor-market weakening indicates a supply-side slowdown that compresses industrial earnings and pressures equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when production softens before unemployment rises.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its prior 3-month average while UNRATE is unchanged or lower
failure_condition: Reject if production declines under the stated trigger are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the pattern disappears once unemployment is controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration in CPIAUCSL combined with a non-easing FEDFUNDS stance raises real-rate pressure and discount rates, which lowers S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when inflation rises but policy rates do not fall in response.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above its prior 6-month average while FEDFUNDS is unchanged or higher
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-up / policy-non-easing episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the relation becomes positive over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in GDPC1 is a broad demand shock that lowers expected corporate revenue and earnings, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal becomes actionable when quarterly real GDP growth turns negative or materially slows relative to the prior quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth turns negative versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if negative-quarter GDPC1 observations are not followed by below-average 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign reverses in more than half of recessionary quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth stalls while UNRATE rises, labor income and confidence weaken together, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as investors reprice cyclical earnings quickly. The combined labor deterioration matters more than either series in isolation.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth falls below zero and UNRATE rises over the same period
failure_condition: Reject if combined PAYEMS weakness and rising UNRATE do not lead to negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST below recent trend signals housing demand destruction and tighter credit transmission, which weakens cyclical earnings expectations and depresses S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should be clearer in housing-led slowdowns than in broad inflation or labor shocks.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% versus its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drops greater than 10% from its 6-month average are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal is absent outside housing-led slowdowns as well as within them.
